rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S To Respond To Written Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, December 29, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Respond To Written Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel for Defendant and moving party Callahan, McCune & Willis are admonished to review and follow the local rules. Courtesy copies of the moving papers were not provided to Department 302, a violation of Local Rule 2.6. Defendant Callahan, McCune & Willis' motion to compel responses Special Interrogatories, Set No. One and Requests for Production of Documents, Set No. One is GRANTED. Plaintiffs are hereby ORDERED to provide complete, verified responses without objections to Special Interrogatories, Set No. One and all responsive documents within their possession, custody and control to the Requests for Production of Documents, Set No. One, both served by Defendant on August 22, 2014. The responses and documents must be served upon counsel for Defendant Callahan, McCune & Willis within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on this motion. Defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $750.00 and must be paid to counsel for Callahan, McCune & Willis within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA MILLER VS. PAUL E. AUGER DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Battery Cause Of Action And Punitive Damages Claim
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 29, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF VICTORIA MILLER's motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Battery Cause Of Action And Punitive Damages Claim The motion is denied. Plaintiff's proposed battery cause of action fails as a matter of law because plaintiff does not allege a touching. See CACI 1300; Cal. Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1983) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280 (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390, 407). Plaintiff withdrew the motion as to the claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC VS. SAFEDOCS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Terry Van Bibber And Safedocs To Special Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,372.50
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, December 29, 2014, Line 3: PLAINTIFF BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Terry Van Bibber And Safedocs To Special Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,372.50. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC's Motion to Compel Further Responses of Terry Van Bibber and Safedocs to Special Interrogatories is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant Terry Van Bibber is ORDERED to provide complete and verified supplemental responses to Special Interrogatories 1-19, including 3(a) providing information dating back to January 2, 2011 where time frames are specified in the requests. Defendant Safedocs, Inc. is ORDERED to provide complete and verified supplemental responses to Special Interrogatories 6,7,9,10,11,17 and 19 providing information dating back to January 2, 2011 where time frames are specified in the requests. Plaintiff's request for sanctions in the amount of $10,372.50 is GRANTED, but reduced to $3,750.00 to be paid jointly by Defendants Terry Van Bibber and Safedocs, Inc. to Plaintiff BA BOTTOM LINE, LLC. The verified supplemental responses and payment of sanctions must be served and payment in the form of a certified check within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AKINTAYO SOTOMI et al VS. ADA H.C. KUNG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 29, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT ADA KUNG 's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the cause of action for libel per quod (referred to by Plaintiff as "libel by innuendo"), the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The statement as a matter of law is an opinion and cannot be proven false or untrue. As to libel per se, the Demurrer is overruled. The statements made in the e-mail attached as Exhibit K could be capable of being understood as either fact or opinion, and therefore is not a question appropriate for disposition on demurrer. The Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for intrusion because dissemination of information legitimately obtained is not a basis for this claim. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend. The Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for publication of private facts as the Complaint sufficiently alleges each element. (See Catsouras v. Dept. of Calif. Highway Patrol (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 856, 904 (J. Aronson concur.opn) [stating that because email is "susceptible to easy and thoughtless forwarding to a larger audience," disclosures by email are likely to satisfy the "public disclosure" requirement].) Furthermore, Defendant's argument regarding the harm element is hugely misplaced and the quote is entirely out of context. There is no issue here related whatsoever to standing in federal courts pursuant to Article III of the U.S. Constitution. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to the fifth cause of action as each element is pled, and Plaintiff is entitled to argue causes of action in the alternative irrespective of whether they are based on the same alleged facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg
